description: Official letter from the Yeshiva to a notable whose name is torn away. In Hebrew for the exordium and Judaeo-Arabic for the body. Dating: 11th or 12th century. Wide space between the lines. The addressee had ignored the Yeshiva's prior letters from both Purim and Pesaḥ. Reused for writing exercises on both sides, including alphabetical writing exercises in a rudimentary hand, the alphabet forwards, backwards and in atbash. (Information in part from CUDL)
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
